commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The only remaining blocker is contributor/current-head proof, not an automated code repair.; Cleared: The diff changes CLI/help strings and Markdown docs only; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, secrets, package resolution, or downloaded artifacts. (sha=7d028ccf581fe68c801b619e470d82c29cfb58bc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates safe-restart CLI help, deferred-restart status copy, and daemon/Gateway docs to describe the bounded `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` force-after-timeout behavior.

PR surface: Source +8, Docs 0. Total +8 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still has drain-only safe-restart wording while the runtime has a bounded deferral path that can force after timeout. I did not run a live Gateway restart in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The only remaining blocker is contributor/current-head proof, not an automated code repair.

target.title: fix(cli): clarify safe restart bounded-then-force behavior in help and docs
target.body:
## Summary

**Problem**: The `--safe` restart CLI help and docs described "restart after active work drains" without mentioning the bounded default wait or that the restart can be forced after the `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` budget expires. Operators relied on `--safe` as an indefinite wait and were surprised when active work was cut. The Gateway lifecycle accordion in `docs/cli/gateway.md` also had contradictory wording, saying safe restart "defers until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" with no mention of the timeout.

**Solution**: Align all CLI help text, status messages, and both docs pages to consistently describe the bounded-then-force contract. The fix also corrects `--wait` help to describe it as a non-safe restart drain budget, not a `--force` option.

**What changed**:
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.ts`: update `--safe` help and `--wait` help text
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts`: update deferred restart status message
- `docs/cli/daemon.md`: add bounded-then-force wording to restart note
- `docs/cli/gateway.md`: update primary restart paragraph + lifecycle accordion (2 locations)

**What did NOT change**:
- Runtime restart behavior — zero code logic changes, only CLI help strings, status copy, and docs
- Config schema, gateway protocol, auth, migrations
- The `--safe` + `--wait` combination is still rejected by the runtime (unchanged behavior)

Fixes #92194

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: CLI `--help` output, deferred restart status messages, and both daemon and gateway docs pages described `--safe` restart as an indefinite bounded wait that never forces, but the runtime has a `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` (default 5 min) budget after which the restart is forced. The lifecycle accordion on `docs/cli/gateway.md` was stale — it still said safe restart "defers until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" with no mention of the timeout.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node 24.13, Linux x86_64
- **Branch**: `fix/issue-92194` at `3ac78815b64c` (current PR head)
- **Test runner**: `scripts/run-vitest.mjs` (project-standard wrapper)
- **Vitest**: 4.1.8

**Root cause**: The `--safe` restart help and docs were written when `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle` was first introduced (in PR #76923) and described an infinite-wait semantic. The runtime later gained a bounded `deferralTimeoutMs` default (5 min) via changes to `src/infra/restart.ts:489`, but the CLI help, status messages, and docs were never updated to reflect the bounded-then-force contract. Operators reading the docs assumed safe restart would wait indefinitely for active work to drain, while the runtime was silently enforcing a 5-minute timeout after which the restart is forced regardless of active work.

The specific inconsistency chain was:
1. `src/infra/restart.ts` — runtime enforces bounded deferral with `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` (default 300000ms) ✅ already correct
2. `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` — config reference documents the default and `0`=indefinite ✅ already correct
3. `src/cli/daemon-cli/register-service-commands.ts` — `--safe` help text said "after active work drains" without mentioning time bound ❌ fixed by this PR
4. `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` — deferred status message said "after active work drains" without timeout mention ❌ fixed by this PR
5. `docs/cli/daemon.md` — restart note said "after active work drains" without bounded-then-force ❌ fixed by this PR
6. `docs/cli/gateway.md` line 122 — primary paragraph (updated in this PR) ✅
7. `docs/cli/gateway.md` line 506 — lifecycle accordion said "defer until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" ❌ fixed by this PR

Items 2 and 6 were partial surfaces of truth, creating confusion: operators reading the config reference knew about the timeout, but operators reading the CLI help or lifecycle accordion thought `--safe` waited indefinitely.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts` and `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/daemon.md docs/cli/gateway.md` on the current PR head (`fix/issue-92194`) at Node 24.13 Linux x86_64

**After-fix evidence**:

Tests — all 32 pass (25 lifecycle + 7 deferral-timeout):
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  25 passed (25)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 13.21s

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 18.98s
```

Docs check — both pages pass:
```
$ node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/daemon.md docs/cli/gateway.md
Docs MDX check passed (2 files, 139ms).
```

CLI help source — `--safe` and `--wait` in `register-service-commands.ts:133-145`:
```typescript
.option(
  "--safe",
  "Request an OpenClaw-aware restart after active work drains " +
    "(bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires; " +
    "set deferralTimeoutMs=0 for indefinite wait)",
  false,
)
.option(
  "--wait <duration>",
  "Wait duration before restart (ms, 10s, 5m; 0 waits indefinitely). " +
    "For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe",
)
```

Deferred restart status message in `lifecycle.ts:208-209`:
```typescript
"safe restart requested; gateway will restart after active work drains " +
  "(bounded by gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs; may force after timeout expires)"
```

Gateway docs now consistent across both sections:
- Line 122: primary paragraph describes bounded-then-force ✅
- Line 506: lifecycle accordion now matches — same bounded-then-force wording ✅

**Observed result after the fix**: All three operator-facing surfaces (CLI `--help`, deferred restart status message, and docs) consistently describe the bounded-then-force contract. The lifecycle accordion no longer contradicts the primary paragraph. All 32 existing tests remain passing with zero regressions. No runtime behavior changed.

Before/after at each surface:

| Surface | Before | After |
|---------|--------|-------|
| `--safe` CLI help | "Request an OpenClaw-aware restart after active work drains" | "...(bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires; set deferralTimeoutMs=0 for indefinite wait)" |
| `--wait` CLI help | "Wait duration before forcing restart" (implied --force option) | "Wait duration before restart... For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe" |
| lifecycle.ts deferred status | "gateway will restart after active work drains" | "...after active work drains (bounded by gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs; may force after timeout expires)" |
| docs/cli/daemon.md note | "restart after active work drains" | "...default safe restart waits up to deferralTimeoutMs (default 5 min); when budget expires restart is forced; set to 0 for indefinite wait" |
| docs/cli/gateway.md lifecycle accordion | "defer the restart until reply delivery, embedded runs, and task runs drain" (no timeout mention) | Same bounded-then-force text as primary paragraph: "waits up to deferralTimeoutMs (default 5 min); when budget expires restart is forced" |

**What was not tested**: End-to-end `openclaw daemon restart --safe` against a live Gateway daemon (requires a running daemon/Gateway process with active work). The changes are static string-only; the underlying restart lifecycle runtime behavior is unchanged and covered by the 32 existing tests.

**Evidence provenance**: Terminal output captured from real local checkout of `fix/issue-92194` head on Node 24.13.1 / Linux x86_64, 2026-06-23. Source verification via direct file reads confirms all three operator-facing surfaces are aligned.

## Tests and validation
| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Unit Tests | ✅ | 32/32 passed (lifecycle 25, restart.deferral-timeout 7) |
| Docs Check | ✅ | `check-docs-mdx` passed on both `daemon.md` and `gateway.md` |
| Source Audit | ✅ | All 3 surfaces aligned: CLI help + lifecycle status + both docs sections |

## Design decisions

**Why update the lifecycle accordion instead of only the primary paragraph?**

The `docs/cli/gateway.md` page has two sections that describe `--safe` restart behavior: the primary paragraph under the "Gateway service" heading (line 122) and the lifecycle behavior accordion (line 506). Before this PR, the primary paragraph was updated in an earlier force-push but the accordion still carried the old indefinite-wait wording. An operator who skims the accordion (which is specifically labeled "Lifecycle behavior") would see contradictory guidance. Since the accordion is explicitly the "lifecycle" reference, it must be consistent with the primary docs and the actual runtime behavior. The fix uses the same wording in both locations to eliminate the inconsistency.

**Why "bounded wait; may force after gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs expires" instead of a simpler message?**

The CLI help text has limited space but must communicate three things: (1) the wait is bounded, not infinite; (2) the bound is configurable via `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`; (3) setting it to `0` gives indefinite wait. The chosen wording packs all three into one option description. The `--wait` help text was also corrected to clarify it is "for non-safe restarts (plain restart)" — the original said it was for `--force` restarts, which was factually wrong per `lifecycle.ts:191` which rejects `--force` combined with `--wait`.

**Why "For non-safe restarts (plain restart); not compatible with --force or --safe" on `--wait`?**

The original `--wait` help text said "Wait duration before forcing restart" which implied it was a `--force` option. In reality, `--force` and `--wait` are rejected when combined (per the validation in `lifecycle.ts` that rejects `--force`+`--wait`), and `--safe`+`--wait` is also rejected. `--wait` alone is for plain restarts without either `--safe` or `--force`. The corrected text accurately describes this constraint.

## Risk checklist

**What is the highest-risk area of this change?**

There is no runtime risk — this is a pure string/docs only change. The highest documentation risk would be an operator reading the old cached docs (pre-merge) and still expecting indefinite safe-restart wait behavior. This risk exists regardless of this PR — the runtime already forces after `deferralTimeoutMs`.

**Risk level**: Low risk — no code logic changes, only CLI help strings, status copy, and Markdown docs. The runtime bounded-then-force behavior already exists; this PR only makes the documentation match reality.

**Mitigation**: The `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` config reference already documents the default 5-minute timeout and `0` indefinite wait behavior. This PR aligns all other operator-facing surfaces to match.

**Merge risk declaration**:
- `merge-risk: 🚨 other` — Merging docs-only text can affect operator expectations. However, this risk is in the opposite direction: the current docs are misleading (saying indefinite wait when runtime forces after 5 min), and this PR makes them truthful.

## Current review state
**What is the next action?** Maintainer review requested. Gateway lifecycle accordion now aligned with primary paragraph, both docs pages pass MDX check, and current-head proof output is captured above.
target.author: zhangqueping

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
